name: Inner-City Sky
milkyWayVisibility: None — sky glows bright enough that many people have never seen the Milky Way
nearestDarkSkyReserves: Cherry Springs State Park (400km from NYC)
description: The most light-polluted skies on Earth, found in the centers of major cities. Only the Moon, bright planets, and a handful of first-magnitude stars (Sirius, Vega, Arcturus) are visible. The entire sky is uniformly bright — orange, white, or bluish depending on local lighting technology. Many urban residents have never seen the Milky Way and may never have seen more than a dozen stars. The night sky provides virtually no astronomical information to the unaided observer. Over 1 billion people live under Class 9 conditions. LED lighting conversion in many cities is actually worsening blue-light sky glow despite energy savings.
